description: Catalog corporate governance mechanisms — reporting, voting, covenants, board oversight, regulation — and assess effectiveness in resolving stakeholder conflicts

fields.name: mechanism
fields.label: reporting | shareholder_voting | creditor_covenant | board_oversight | employee | customer_supplier | regulatory

mechanisms.reporting: Periodic financial and non-financial disclosure — auditors, regulators, standards
mechanisms.shareholder_voting: Board elections, say on pay, major transactions, proxy
mechanisms.creditor_covenants: Affirmative and negative covenants in loan agreements; cross-default
mechanisms.board_oversight: Independent directors, committees, CEO succession, strategy oversight
mechanisms.employee: Collective bargaining, whistleblower protection, works councils
mechanisms.regulatory: Legal frameworks, enforcement, anti-corruption, market conduct

board_structure.dual_role: Chair and CEO separation reduces concentration of power

governance_risks.operational: Fraud, poor strategy execution, reputational incidents
governance_risks.legal_reputational: Litigation, regulatory fines, consumer trust loss
governance_risks.financial: Mispricing of risk, funding cost shocks, distressed financing

errors.message: mechanism must be from the allowed set

anti_patterns.rule: Do not confuse compliance with effective governance
anti_patterns.why: Checklist compliance often coexists with weak culture; probe independence, incentives, and escalation quality

anti_patterns.rule: Do not rely only on board independence count
anti_patterns.why: Directors can be nominally independent yet captured through long tenure or cross-board relationships

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore creditor governance in leveraged firms
anti_patterns.why: Covenants and inter-creditor agreements drive outcomes when equity loses control in distress

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat governance as static
anti_patterns.why: Crises reveal governance gaps; track board refreshment, incidents, and restated accounts over time
